title: So what happened to Asuka
wikitext:
[]: So what happened to Asuka...             Quote Post by narcosis219 » Wed Aug 02, 2006 6:24 pm so first she gets mindraped by that angel, then she starts hating everyone... then she finds out Kaji died (that coffee scene with Shinji) and she ends up in a bathtub with no clothes on. what was she doing naked in that bathtub in some war torn building?

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Wed Aug 02, 2006 6:40 pm She hates everyone because she can no longer pilot eva, which is followed by depression, and eventually she goes into a comatose position. If you've seen the directors cut, you notice that she is frustrated because she has affection (I'm pretty sure) for Shinji, but he's too afraid to show any back,

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Aug 02, 2006 7:03 pm It's pretty obvious to anyone that she went out there to die.

[]:             Quote Post by Shadow » Wed Aug 02, 2006 8:16 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: She hates everyone because she can no longer pilot eva, which is followed by depression, and eventually she goes into a comatose position. If you've seen the directors cut, you notice that she is frustrated because she has affection (I'm pretty sure) for Shinji, but he's too afraid to show any back,

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Wed Aug 02, 2006 8:30 pm Showing affection? I said she has affection for him, not that she ever shows it. It becomes really obvious by episode 22. (Director's cut)

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Aug 03, 2006 12:50 am Asuka didn't hate everyone-- she hated herself, and that was how she vented. She thought that Evangelion was the only thing that gave her any worth, and so when that was gone she felt like no-one needed her, and lost her will to live. She was emotionally broken, and she didn't care if she died or not, because she thought no-one else would care. She felt un-wanted, and after being broken by Arael, she went off the deep end.

[]: Re: So what happened to Asuka...             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 03, 2006 1:09 am narcosis219 wrote: what was she doing naked in that bathtub in some war torn building?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Thu Aug 03, 2006 4:22 am Timstuff wrote: Asuka didn't hate everyone-- she hated herself, and that was how she vented. She thought that Evangelion was the only thing that gave her any worth, and so when that was gone she felt like no-one needed her, and lost her will to live. She was emotionally broken, and she didn't care if she died or not, because she thought no-one else would care. She felt un-wanted, and after being broken by Arael, she went off the deep end.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Aug 04, 2006 1:52 am Saying you hate everyone is an exhibit A symptom of self-loathing. If you hate yourself, how are you supposed to love other people? Even if you do love them, you won't be able to express it since your heart is already filled with nothing but hate and self-doubt. That's what Asuka was demonstrating. And I've always been confused as to wether Asuka had cut herself, or if there was just some rust int he bottom of the bathtub. There was an IV tube taped to her left writst in EoE, but in that picture, the "red" is near her right wrist. If she had attempted suicide then they most definately would have bandaged the place where she'd cut herself, or there'd at least be some kind of visible scar. The only thing near her wrists was the IV, so without any signs of cutting on her writsts, the red in the tub is looking a lot more like rust. While it's possible that she did slit her wrists, I do not know for certain. It's also a possibility that Asuka had simply lost the will to live but didn't have the nerve to commit suicide, so she simply went to that house and decided to wait for death to take her, by whatever means it chose. She was really screwed up in the head at that point, so we don't really know what she was thinking aside from all of her self-loathing and feelings of uselessness. When she got into that bathtub, her intention might not have been to kill herself, but just plain to die. Those might sound like they're the same thing, but they're not. Asuke had literally found someplace quiet to curl up and die, and that's what she was doing when we saw her. She was dying, but I don't think she gave herself any assistance. Really though, either way I end up feeling just as sad for her. :(

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Fri Aug 04, 2006 2:13 am Legend of the Past wrote: Timstuff wrote: Asuka didn't hate everyone-- she hated herself, and that was how she vented. She thought that Evangelion was the only thing that gave her any worth, and so when that was gone she felt like no-one needed her, and lost her will to live. She was emotionally broken, and she didn't care if she died or not, because she thought no-one else would care. She felt un-wanted, and after being broken by Arael, she went off the deep end.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 04, 2006 2:24 am FauÃŸtin wrote: Me too. I can still recall the heart-gripping sadness; ... Why isn't there somebody to tell her that she is important?" :cry: :(

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Aug 04, 2006 3:26 am Mr. Tines wrote: FauÃŸtin wrote: Me too. I can still recall the heart-gripping sadness; ... Why isn't there somebody to tell her that she is important?" :cry: :(

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Fri Aug 04, 2006 12:35 pm And you can imagine how I felt when Shinji started to ... well, you know what I'm going to say, so I'll just save space/time/energy by finishing this sentence. Anno is a genius when it comes to demoralizing the audience. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Aug 04, 2006 3:38 pm What's even more depressing, is that you can tell that Shinji is thinking all the same things as the audience. He feels just as broken up about Asuka as we all do, and possibly even more since he knew her personally and was very emotional emotionally attached. Not only that, but he needed to look up to Asuka as being someone stronger than him, and seeing her like that probably tore him apart. But then, her shirt had to go and pop open, and everything went downhill from there. :oops:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 04, 2006 3:43 pm Things were already going downhill with all the hurt he'd been doing to her, without even realising what he'd been doing.

[]:             Quote Post by Auska » Mon Aug 07, 2006 7:21 pm So she did die or what?

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Aug 07, 2006 9:16 pm Of course she didn't die or you wouldn't see her fight the Harpies.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 08, 2006 1:18 am Asuka wrote: So she did die or what?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Aug 08, 2006 1:35 am Yup, getting eaten alive isn't a very fun way to go, but go it she did. :(

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Tue Aug 08, 2006 4:16 am But with such a strong will to live she is "alive" again after the rejection of Instrumentality.

Article: So what happened to Asuka
 I do not know for certain. It's also a possibility that Asuka had simply lost the will to live but didn't have the nerve to commit suicide, so she simply went to that house and decided to wait for death to take her, by whatever means it chose. She was really screwed up in the head at that point, so we don't really know what she was thinking aside from all of her self-loathing and feelings of uselessness. When she got into that bathtub, her intention might not have been to kill herself, but just plain to die. Those might sound like they're the same thing, but they're not. Asuke had literally found someplace quiet to curl up and die, and that's what she was doing when we saw her. She was dying, but I don't think she gave herself any assistance. Really though, either way I end up feeling just as sad for her. :(

She thought that Evangelion was the only thing that gave her any worth, and so when that was gone she felt like no-one needed her, and lost her will to live. She was emotionally broken, and she didn't care if she died or not, because she thought no-one else would care. She felt un-wanted, and after being broken by Arael, she went off the deep end.
